_setup_notes.post_import_step: After importing: 1) Open the 'Execute: Core Workflow' node, click the workflow selector, and choose 'Speed to Lead — Core' by name. This updates the CORE_WORKFLOW_ID placeholder to the actual workflow ID assigned by this n8n instance. 2) Set env vars: DUPONT_GOOGLE_KEY, DUPONT_TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID, DUPONT_TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN, DUPONT_TWILIO_SENDER_ID. 3) Activate the workflow. 4) Configure Google Ads Lead Form webhook URL to: https://{your-n8n-host}/webhook/dupont-plomberie
_setup_notes.google_key_note: Per-client google_key validation happens here in the entry workflow — the Core Workflow trusts the payload it receives from authenticated entry workflows.
_setup_notes.fire_and_forget: waitForSubWorkflow: false is REQUIRED. The Core Workflow contains a Wait node (45 min by default). If waitForSubWorkflow were true, this entry workflow would block for 45+ minutes before returning HTTP 200 to Google Ads — causing timeout and lead loss.
